questions.text: What was everyone talking about in the entire kingdom?
questions.explanation: It was the season for Hilsa fish, and fishermen and courtiers could talk of nothing else.

questions.text: Why was the King annoyed with his courtiers?
options: They were only talking about Hilsa fish
questions.explanation: The King lost his temper because even the courtiers were talking about fish instead of important matters.

questions.text: What challenge did the King give to Gopal?
options: To catch the biggest Hilsa fish
To bring a Hilsa fish to the palace without anyone asking about it
To cook Hilsa fish for the palace
To stop the people from fishing
questions.explanation: The King challenged Gopal to buy a huge Hilsa and bring it to the court without a single person asking a word about it.

questions.text: Gopal prepared himself by shaving only half his face and smearing himself with:
questions.explanation: Gopal made himself look like a madman by using ash and wearing rags.
She was shocked by his disgraceful appearance and thought he had lost his mind.

questions.text: When Gopal reached the palace with the fish, the guards:
questions.explanation: The guards didn't recognize him and wouldn't let the 'madman' enter until he started singing and dancing loudly.

questions.text: Inside the palace, what did the King's courtiers think of Gopal?
questions.explanation: They thought he was crazy or that it was one of his clever jokes.

questions.text: Did anyone ask Gopal about the Hilsa fish on his way to the palace?
questions.explanation: Everyone was so busy looking at his strange appearance that they forgot to notice the fish.

questions.text: What was the King's reaction when he realized Gopal had won the challenge?
questions.explanation: The King laughed and admitted that Gopal had achieved the impossible once again.

questions.text: The story 'Gopal and the Hilsa Fish' is written in the form of a:
questions.explanation: The chapter uses a visual comic strip format to tell the story.
